title: Consenti Agreement Protocol v0.1
description: Canonical schema for cryptographically verifiable terms acceptance — by humans or agents — with third-party proof. Transport-agnostic. Chain-agnostic. Zero-custody by construction. Terms before transactions.

agreement_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this acceptance record. SHOULD be a UUIDv7 or ULID for time-orderability.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp after which this acceptance is no longer in force. Null = no expiry.

publisher.description: The entity that published the terms — the company, service, or platform whose T&C are being accepted.

display_name.description: Human-readable publisher name for dashboard display.

acceptor.description: The party that accepted the terms. Can be a human or an agent. Both produce identical log entries in the dashboard.

acceptor_type.description: Whether the acceptor is a human user or an AI agent. Determines display grouping in the dashboard but does not affect verification.

type.description: Identifier scheme. Humans typically identified by email, IP, or session. Agents by agent_id, DID, or URL.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for dashboard display (e.g., 'John Smith', 'Stripe Payment Agent v2.1').

ip_address.description: IP address at time of acceptance (human acceptors). Null for agents.

user_agent.description: Browser user-agent string (human acceptors) or agent framework identifier (agent acceptors).

terms_ref.description: Reference to the specific version of terms that were accepted. Addressed by content hash so the exact version is always recoverable.

terms_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized terms document. Immutable link between acceptance record and exact terms version.

terms_url.description: URL where the terms were published at time of acceptance.

terms_version.description: Human-readable version label (e.g., 'v4.2', '2026-04-01'). Informational — terms_hash is authoritative.

terms_title.description: Title of the terms document for dashboard display (e.g., 'API Terms of Service', 'Data Processing Agreement').

acceptance_method.description: How the acceptor indicated acceptance. 'checkbox_click' and 'button_click' for human web flows. 'agent_protocol' for agents accepting via the Consenti protocol. 'api_commit' for programmatic acceptance.

proof_of_understanding.description: Optional PoU attestation. Demonstrates comprehension of terms by the acceptor. Available when Encrypted Cloud Mode is enabled.

method.description: How understanding was verified. 'ai_assistant_qa' = acceptor asked questions of an AI assistant about the terms before accepting. 'key_points_checklist' = acceptor confirmed each key point.

evidence_hash.description: Hash of attestation evidence (Q&A transcript, checklist confirmations, quiz responses). Evidence held by the service, not on-chain.

score.description: Normalized score 0.0–1.0 if method produces one.

jurisdiction.description: Declared governing law. Informational for dashboard; dispositive for dispute resolution.

governing_law.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, optionally with subdivision (e.g., 'US-DE', 'GB', 'ES').

anchoring.description: Chain-agnostic anchor metadata. This is the third-party proof — the independent, tamper-proof record that acceptance happened. It is your word AND an independent record, not just your word. Null until anchored.

chain_id.description: Chain identifier where applicable (e.g., EIP-155 chain ID for EVM).

transaction_ref.description: Transaction hash, TSA token, or equivalent anchor reference.

agreement_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized acceptance record (excluding anchoring, signatures, and privacy derived fields).

signatures.description: Optional cryptographic signatures. Required for agent acceptors. For human checkbox/click acceptance, the anchored record itself serves as proof. Excluded from agreement_hash computation.

public_key_ref.description: DID verification method, JWK thumbprint, or key reference.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded signature over the canonical agreement hash.

privacy.description: Privacy and confidentiality controls. See companion spec: agreement-privacy-architecture.md.

model.description: Privacy model. 'public' is default for standard T&C acceptance. 'selective_disclosure' for sensitive agreements.

merkle_tree.description: Clause-level Merkle tree for selective disclosure.

anchor_commitment.description: What is committed to the public anchor. Only hashes — never plaintext.

canonicalization.description: How the acceptance record is canonicalized prior to hashing and signing. MUST be deterministic.

excluded_fields.description: Fields excluded from canonicalization prior to hashing.

extensions.description: Namespace for protocol extensions. Unknown extensions MUST NOT affect verification.
